## SECTION 8 — This Day in History (World)
On August 12, 1981, the incredibly massive, fundamentally historic, and globally defining IBM Personal Computer (IBM PC) was officially and aggressively released to the public. Heavily featuring an incredibly open architecture and aggressively utilizing the massive Microsoft MS-DOS operating system, this massive, groundbreaking machine absolutely revolutionized global computing. It fundamentally democratized computing power, heavily shifting the massive technology from exclusive corporate mainframes directly onto the massive, domestic desks of millions of global consumers.

On August 12, 1898, the incredibly massive, deeply strategic, and heavily momentous Spanish-American War effectively and aggressively concluded. Following the massive, overwhelming naval and land victories of the United States Armed Forces, Spanish and American officials heavily signed a massive Protocol of Peace in Washington, D.C. This massive armistice absolutely forced the deeply crumbling Spanish Empire to aggressively relinquish its absolute sovereignty over Cuba and cede the massive, highly strategic territories of Puerto Rico and Guam to the ascendant American empire.

## SECTION 9 — This Day in History (India)
On August 12, 1919, Vikram Sarabhai, the incredibly massive, absolutely brilliant, and universally revered "Father of the Indian Space Program," was officially born in Ahmedabad. A massive, visionary physicist and industrialist, he aggressively and fundamentally established the incredibly massive Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO). His absolute, uncompromising belief that space technology must be heavily utilized to aggressively solve the massive, real-world developmental problems of deeply impoverished nations absolutely defined the massive, enduring ethos of India's incredibly successful aerospace endeavors.

On August 12, 1765, the incredibly massive, deeply historic, and heavily catastrophic Treaty of Allahabad was officially and aggressively signed. Following the massive, decisive British military victory at the Battle of Buxar, the deeply defeated Mughal Emperor Shah Alam II was forced to aggressively grant the absolute *Diwani* rights (massive tax collection authority) of Bengal, Bihar, and Orissa to the British East India Company. This massive, monumental document fundamentally legitimized and heavily initiated the absolute, massive plundering of the subcontinent by British colonial administrators.
